extraction_reasoning: The litigation tables distinguish matters against and by each entity. I counted only against rows: criminal proceedings against the Company (11), subsidiaries (1), promoters (10) and directors (9), totaling 31; statutory/regulatory actions against the Company (1), subsidiaries (1) and promoters (1), totaling 3; and direct tax proceedings against the Company (5) and subsidiaries (4), totaling 9. For the quantified aggregate against the Company and subsidiaries, I used the against rows only: Company ₹77,721.85 million civil + ₹19.00 million regulatory + ₹818.12 million direct tax, and subsidiaries ₹9,859.21 million civil + ₹25.37 million regulatory + ₹151.07 million direct tax = ₹88,594.62 million, or ₹8,859.462 crore; matters by those entities and personal promoter/director matters were excluded. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated Total of ₹7,821.69 million as of December 31, 2020, converted to ₹782.169 crore rather than re-summed. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer concentration percentage is disclosed; the only quantified percentages in the section relate to project geography, compensation as a percentage of aggregate revenue from operations, or other non-customer metrics. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
